LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF MARCUS VAN RAALTE'S MOTION TO AMEND JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 10, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MAY 14, 2013 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. PROOF OF SERVICE OF JUDGMENT; DECLARATION RE INTEREST; DECLARATION PURSUANT TO 585 CCP; DECLARATION OF MARCUS VAN RAALTE; REQUEST FOR COURT JUDGMENT; REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL FILED BY PLAINTIFF VAN RAALTE, MARCUS SERVED MAR-22-2010, MAIL AS TO DEFENDANT ORANGE LEGAL TECHNOLOGIES, LLC
DECLARATION OF MARCUS VAN RAALTE PURSUANT TO 585D CCP FILED BY PLAINTIFF VAN RAALTE, MARCUS
OPPOS TO PLTF'S MOTION TO AMEND JUDGMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT THE LITIGATION DOCUMENT GROUP, LLC , A UTAH LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, DOING BUSINESS AS ORANGE LEGAL TECHNOLOGIES

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF MARCUS VAN RAALTE'S MOTION TO AMEND JUDGMENT IS DENIED. 1) THE COURT FINDS IT NOT CLEAR WHETHER THE COURT HAS JURISDICTION OVER THE DEFENDANTS AND IS NOT SUPPORTED BY EVIDENCE. 2) THE ENTIRE THEORY OF THE MOTION IS ALTER EGO AND THE COURT FINDS THAT THE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN; 3) THIS IS A DEFAULT JUDGMENT WHERE LDG IS NOT A PARTY TO THIS ACTION AND THERE IS INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SHOW THAT IT HAD THE OPPORTUNITY TO REPRESENT ITSELF (SEE MOTORES DE MEXICALI, S. A. V. SUPERIOR COURT IN & FOR LOS ANGELES CNTY., 51 CAL. 2D 172, 176 (1958).) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
DECLARATION RE INTEREST FILED BY PLAINTIFF VAN RAALTE, MARCUS
DEFAULT JUDGMENT REJECTED, PLACED IN PICKUP BOX, SUBMITTED BY PLAINTIFF VAN RAALTE, MARCUS AS TO DEFENDANT ORANGE LEGAL TECHNOLOGIES, LLC
NOTICE OF CHANGE OF FIRM NAME FILED BY ATTORNEY GELLER, MICHAEL S.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-24-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL S. GELLER, TEL. NO. (951) 248-6455 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. ETHAN CHERNIN OF BALLARD SPAHR LLP, TEL. NO. (424) 204-4400 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT DOCUMENT GROUP BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF MARCUS VAN RAALTE'S Motion To Amend Judgment is Denied. 1) THE COURT FINDS IT NOT CLEAR WHETHER THE COURT HAS JURISDICTION OVER THE DEFENDANTS AND IS NOT SUPPORTED BY EVIDENCE. 2) THE ENTIRE THEORY OF THE MOTION IS ALTER EGO AND THE COURT FINDS THAT THE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN; 3) THIS IS A DEFAULT JUDGMENT WHERE LDG IS NOT A PARTY TO THIS ACTION AND THERE IS INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SHOW THAT IT HAD THE OPPORTUNITY TO REPRESENT ITSELF (See Motores De Mexicali, S. A. v. Superior Court In & For Los Angeles Cnty., 51 Cal. 2d 172, 176 (1958).) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-10-2013 Continued To Jun-24-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MARCUS VAN RAALTE'S Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment is Continued on the court's own motion June 24, 2013. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment
Off Calendar May-14-2013 Continued To Jun-10-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MARCUS VAN RAALTE's Motion To Amend Judgment is continued to June 10, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 14, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
